title: The Secret Purge
subtitle: Erase the forbidden key from all of time

briefing.narrative:
Code red. CODE RED.

Someone -- and the monastery security log DEFINITELY knows who -- committed the monastery's API key directly into the repository. Commit number two of five. It's been sitting there in .env like a neon sign saying "HACK ME."

The Head Monk is doing that thing where he's so calm it's terrifying. "Remove it," he says quietly. "Not just from the current tree. From ALL of history. As if it was never there."

You need to go back in time, rebuild history without the tainted commit, and cherry-pick the good work forward. The API key must vanish from existence.

The monastery's security depends on you. Also, the monk who committed it left a apologetic cookie on your desk.
briefing.concept: Sometimes you need to completely remove a file from Git history -- not just delete it in a new commit (it would still be in old commits). One approach: reset to before the bad commit, recommit cleanly, then cherry-pick the good commits that came after.
objectives: Go back to before that commit
Cherry-pick the remaining good commits forward
Create a .gitignore to prevent .env from being committed again

hints.text: The security monk whispers: 'Use git log to find commit hashes. You need to reset to before the .env commit, then cherry-pick the commits that came after it.'
A classified scroll reads: 'Reset to before the tainted commit. Then rebuild history by cherry-picking only the clean commits forward.'
